reasoning: The resolution condition requires Canada to experience more than 10 times the baseline rate of Explosions/Remote violence in a 30 day period. The provided reference value shows the baseline is around 1 event per 360 days, meaning the threshold to resolve YES is 11 or more such events in a 30-day window. Historically, ACLED lists almost no events of this type (which involves artillery, IEDs, airstrikes, or large bombings) in Canada, and comprehensive search of recent news through October 26, 2025 shows zero incidents that would qualify. Reaching 11 events would represent an unprecedented outbreak of a terrorist campaign or armed conflict and is thus incredibly unlikely. The prediction is set to the minimum allowable probability (0.05) for the near-term dates and slightly higher (0.06) for the later date due to the marginally larger uncertainty window.
